entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
Students read about important events and influential people from Church History using biographies and narrative-driven overviews of the time period. In this course, students delve into the 19th century and learn the stories of a variety of Christians.

This topic is included in the following course(s): Bible: Grades 7-8, Bible: Grade 9

rows.label: Church History: Grades 7-9
rows.books: Spurgeon: A Biography||Renewal: The Church That Expands Outward||An Honorable Heritage: The Pandita Ramabai Story in Her Own Words

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

books.title: Renewal: The Church That Expands Outward
An Honorable Heritage: The Pandita Ramabai Story in Her Own Words

blocks.text: Connect back to the previous lesson. Ask students to give a summary of what they remember. (Full narration is not necessary.)
Use a picture, a map, or an engaging question to prepare students to receive the story. You may talk about one or two unfamiliar words that will be crucial to understanding.
Optional: Share something interesting from your preparation that will help shed light on the story.
Give students an opportunity to share their thoughts on the story and any connections made.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
William Wilberforce
⍞ Materials: Renewal: The Church that Expands Outward, notebook

➜ INTRO
1. This year we are learning about people who lived during the 19th century (1800-1900).  We will read how these Christians lived out Jesus' commands. When Jesus was asked what the greatest command was, he replied, "You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind.** **This is the great and first commandment.** **And a second is like it: You shall love your neighbor as yourself." (Matthew 22:37-39)
2. 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.93-100 (whole chapter)
lessons.teacherNotes:
IMPORTANT DATES
WIlliam Wilberforce 1759-1833
Slave Trade was abolished in England 1833

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 13
lessons.body:
The Boy and the Books
⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography, book of centuries

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about your reading last time in Church History? 

➜ INTRO
The next book we will be reading in Church History is a biography about Charles H. Spurgeon. Born in 1834, Spurgeon was a pastor and author who lived a life of faithful ministry. His devotional writings are still read by many today. 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch. 1 p.3-12
Optional: "Conditions in England During Spurgeon's Time" p.xv-xvii

➜ NOTE \*\*\*\*WHAT IS THE CORRECT PROMPT\*\*\*
Enter Spurgeon's birthdate into your book of centuries.
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
Charles H. Spurgeon born June 19, 1834

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 14
lessons.body:
Conversion & Joy in Serving
⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon last time? 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.15-27
•On p.21 the author makes a statement that reads like a thesis statement or main point of what will be coming in the book: "The failure of preachers he had heard to present the gospel, and to do so in a plain, direct manner, caused him throughout his whole ministry to tell sinners in every sermon and in a most forthright and understandable way how to be saved." How do you think having this goal or idea in mind helped Spurgeon in his ministry? As we continue reading, note in what ways he stays consistent with this important point in his ministry.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 15
lessons.body:
The Boy Preacher
 ⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography

➜ RECAP 
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon from last time? 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.28-29; 31-40
•The author divides up the book into four main parts, with today's reading closing out the first part of the book. What are three main events from these early years of Spurgeon's life that stood out to you that seem to be preparing him for a life of ministry?

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 16
lessons.body:
Called to Be a Pastor
 ⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography, book of centuries

➜ RECAP 
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon from last time? 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.43-53

➜ VIEW
⍞ Image Resource: Spurgeon: A Biography - images located after p.87
View the first four pages of pictures in your book (located after p.87). Look at the image of Spurgeon at the age of 20 (around the time of being called as a pastor). Do you think those who put him down for his young age had a point? Why or why not?

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 18
lessons.body:
The Church Grows
 ⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography

NOTE: Today's reading is a bit shorter. Use any extra time to catch up on reading or to read the article at the link. 

➜ RECAP 
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon from last time? 

➜ INTRO
Spurgeon placed importance on prayer and on the people who were attending his church. Their conversion was a deep desire of his heart. The church he led put into place some ways of following up with those who professed salvation and who wanted to be members. As you read, consider how these ideas would work in a modern church today. Would any of the ideas be helpful? Would any not work in a modern church? Why or why not? 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.81-87 "His was a"-"great Metropolitan Temple."
•Do you think any of the ideas the church implemented to follow up with those who wanted to be members were helpful? Would any of those ideas work in a modern church? Why or why not? 

➜ Optional: Read the linked article containing a visitor's experience hearing Spurgeon preach. 
∞ Article Link: What It Was Like to Hear Charles Spurgeon Preach

➜ VIEW
Take some time to look at the rest of the images included in the book (located after p.87). What do you notice?

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 19
lessons.body:
The Metropolitan Tabernacle
⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography; book of centuries

➜ RECAP 
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon from last time? 

➜ INTRO
The Metropolitan Tabernacle would be the church Spurgeon would spend the rest of his days as a preacher. In today's reading, he also will speak to a record-setting crowd of 23,654 at The Crystal Palace, the place he and Susannah had spent so much time. Imagine speaking - with just your voice! - to that large of a crowd! 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.9 p.91-99

➜ VIEW
∞ Image Link: Metropolitan Tabernacle
The link shows the Metropolitan Tabernacle in more recent times. The Tabernacle is still active as a church to this day, although it has had to be rebuilt twice, once due to a fire and then after a bombing during World War II. It has a slightly different design to its front after its latest rebuild.
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
March 31, 1861 - First Sunday Service at the Metropolitan Tabernacle

• EXTRA HELPING
∞ Article Link: Spurgeon's Fast Day Sermon
Read the sermon Spurgeon preached to the record-setting crowd at The Crystal Palace.

 ⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography

➜ RECAP 
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon from last time? 

➜ INTRO 
The next couple of chapters start to show the growth of Spurgeon's ministry outside of his pastorship. First, a college is started to train young pastors.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.10 p.101-109

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 21
lessons.body:
New Ways of Reaching the Community
 ⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography

NOTE: Parts of this chapter will be skipped for the sake of time.

➜ RECAP 
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon from last time? 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.111-113 "In 1865 Spurgeon"-"build God's work."
p.115-122 "But though Spurgeon"-"days on earth."
•The church began reaching out into the community using the publishing of a magazine, printing of sermons, and building new churches. In what ways is the message of the gospel spread by the church you attend? Are any of those ways similar to what the Metropolitan Tabernacle used? In what new (modern) ways does your church use to reach the surrounding community?

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 22
lessons.body:
Widows & Orphans
⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography

➜ RECAP 
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon from last time? 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.12 p.125-130
p.133-137 "From the late"-"of the gout."

James 1:27-"Religion	that is pure and undefiled before God the Father is this: to visit orphans and widows in their affliction, and to keep oneself unstained from the world." 	
•Based on today's reading and the verse above, do you agree or disagree with the author's statement that "The orphanage was a lasting demonstration of the fact that Spurgeon's faith was not mere theory but that it produced good works" (p.130)? Why or why not?

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 23
lessons.body:
Mrs. Spurgeon
 ⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography

➜ RECAP 
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon from last time? 

➜ INTRO
Charles Spurgeon's wife Susannah was a big part of his ministry and today's chapter brings her work into the picture. Mrs. Spurgeon suffered from a medical illness that kept her from traveling or even attending church services for much of her adult life. However, today's reading will show how God brought together her unique abilities to minister to others and enabled her to have an impact on many. 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.137-150 "Of this attack"-"and comfort others."

➜ VIEW
∞ Image Link: Susie Spurgeon

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 26
lessons.body:
Books
 ⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography

➜ RECAP 
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon from last time? 

➜ INTRO
In today's reading, Spurgeon's library of 12,000 books is mentioned. Spurgeon felt it was imperative that those who were teaching the Bible be learned and readers of books. In fact, he gave two pieces of advice about books to students: "1. Buy a few, high-quality books; 2. “Master those books you have.” “Read them thoroughly,” he wrote. “Bathe in them until they saturate you. Read and re-read them, masticate them, and digest them. Let them go into your very self. Peruse a good book several times, and make notes and analyses of it. A student will find that his mental constitution is more affected by one book thoroughly mastered than by twenty books which he has merely skimmed, lapping at them, as the classic proverb puts it ‘As the dogs drink of Nilus.’”

He also is quoted as having said, “Visit many good books, but live in the Bible.” As time allows, view the extra helping link to see a tour of a library that now holds half of his collection of books, including a commentary on Psalms from 1476. 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.16 p.163-175
lessons.teacherNotes:
EXTRA HELPING

View the video at the link to see the library where 6,000 of Spurgeon's books are kept. You'll also see artifacts from Spurgeon's life, including a short recording of his voice.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 29
lessons.body:
Controversy
 ⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography

➜ RECAP 
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon from last time? 

➜ INTRO
There are two parts to your reading today. First, read the assigned pages in the biography of Spurgeon that discuss a controversy that came up. There was a movement called Higher Criticism that moved through churches in the mid-1800s. This movement caused people to have a lower view of the Bible, which Spurgeon was highly motivated to argue against. After reading the pages in the book, read the linked sermon which Spurgeon spoke during this time. You may split the sermon reading between this week and next week to fit the time allotted. 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.19 p.205-209 "The Metropolitan Tabernacle"-"their firm opposition."
∞ Link: "Poverty and Riches" Sermon by Spurgeon 
If needed, the sermon may be divided between this week and next week's lesson time.

lessons.title: 30m Church History: Grades 7-9 - Lesson 30
lessons.body:
Glory Dwelleth in Immanuel's Land
 ⍞ Materials: Spurgeon: A Biography; book of centuries

NOTE: A few pages are skipped at the beginning of the chapter for time's sake. Assigned pages are listed below. 

➜ RECAP 
What do you recall about your reading about Spurgeon from last time? 

➜ INTRO
Toward the end of his life, Spurgeon experienced much physical pain and sickness. However, he held tight to his faith and looked forward to being with his Savior. 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.224-233 "He found relief"-"is far better."
∞ Link: Poverty and Riches Sermon by Spurgeon
Complete your reading of the sermon if needed.
•In the first lesson on the biography of Spurgeon, we noted the author makes a statement that reads like a thesis statement or main point of what Spurgeon strived to keep in mind when telling others about the gospel (p.21): "The failure of preachers he had heard to present the gospel, and to do so in a plain, direct manner, caused him throughout his whole ministry to tell sinners in every sermon and in a most forthright and understandable way how to be saved." Now that you have read more on Spurgeon, do you think he was able to remain faithful to this goal or aim of his ministry? How?
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
January 31, 1892 - Charles H. Spurgeon Dies
